title: 5xx event count (stats)
statement: Count events whose HTTP status is in the 5xx range. Use status filtering and stats count. Return exactly one row.
hint: Filter to 5xx with status>=500, then stats count.

title: Distinct clientip count (dedup)
statement: Count how many distinct clientip values appear. Use dedup and stats count (or stats dc(clientip)). Return exactly one row.
hint: After dedup clientip, stats count; or stats dc(clientip).

title: Top 3 status by frequency (top)
statement: Show the three status values with the highest counts. Use top.
hint: top 3 status returns the top three by count.

title: 5xx counts over time (timechart)
statement: Plot 5xx counts in one-hour buckets over time. Use timechart with span=1h. Expect multiple rows (one per hour).
hint: Filter 5xx with status>=500, then timechart span=1h count.

title: Session count (transaction)
statement:
Extract session_id from Cookie and count how many sessions there are. Use transaction and stats count. Return exactly one row.

Field extraction sample: rex to extract session_id from cookie: rex field=cookie "session_id=(?<session_id>[^;\s]+)"
hint: After rex pulls session_id, group with transaction by session_id, then stats count.
referenceSpl: | rex field=cookie "session_id=(?<session_id>[^;\s]+)" | sort _time | transaction session_id | stats count

title: Geo counts (geostats)
statement:
Extract lat and lon from URI, then aggregate counts with geostats. Use rex and geostats. For geostats count, specify only latfield and longfield.

Field extraction sample: rex to extract lat/lon from uri: rex field=uri "lat=(?<lat>[0-9.]+)&lon=(?<lon>[0-9.]+)"
hint: rex lat/lon, then geostats count latfield=lat longfield=lon.
referenceSpl: | rex field=uri "lat=(?<lat>[0-9.]+)&lon=(?<lon>[0-9.]+)" | where isnotnull(lat) | geostats count latfield=lat longfield=lon

title: eval labels (ok / client / server)
statement: Classify status into ok (<400), client (4xx), and server (5xx), then count events per class. Use eval with case and stats count. Return three rows.
placeholder:
index=tutorial_m4
(classify with case, count by class)

title: Max cumulative sequence (streamstats)
statement: Return the maximum of the running count from streamstats as a single number. Use streamstats and stats max. Return exactly one row.
hint: streamstats count as seq, then stats max.
referenceSpl: | streamstats count as seq | stats max(seq)

title: order_id via rex, top 3
statement:
Extract order_id from uri and show the three most frequent values. Use rex and top. Return three rows.

Extraction sample: rex to extract order_id from uri: rex field=uri "(?<order_id>\d+)$"
hint: rex field=uri end anchor, then top 3 order_id.

title: All requests from IPs that ever returned 5xx (subsearch)
statement: Restrict to clientip that returned at least one 5xx, then count all requests from those IPs in one row. Pass the subsearch inside join [ search ... ], ending with stats count. Return exactly one row.
hint: Outer search on tutorial_a3 joined to subsearch listing clientips with 5xx.
referenceSpl: | join type=inner clientip [ search index=tutorial_a3 status>=500 | fields clientip | dedup clientip ] | stats count

title: Requests slower than per-method average (eventstats)
statement:
From Cookie rt= (milliseconds) build response_ms, then count events slower than the per-method average. Use rex, eventstats, where, and stats count. Return exactly one row.

Extraction sample: rex to extract response_ms from cookie: rex field=cookie "rt=(?<response_ms>[0-9]+)"
hint: eventstats avg(response_ms) by method, where response_ms > avg_ms, stats count.
referenceSpl: | rex field=cookie "rt=(?<response_ms>[0-9]+)" | eventstats avg(response_ms) as avg_ms by method | where response_ms > avg_ms | stats count

title: Daily event counts (bin)
statement: Count events per calendar day. Use bin (or bucket) and stats count. Expect multiple rows (one per day).
hint: bin _time span=1d then stats count by _time.
